# open-cycle

open-cycle is a free, privacy-first period tracking app.

The app helps people record period dates, flow, symptoms, moods, and notes. It is designed for simple everyday tracking, not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

Core privacy position:
- No account is required for cycle tracking.
- No ads, ad SDKs, hidden analytics, or required cloud sync are used for core tracking.
- Cycle entries are stored locally on the user's phone or browser.
- The app does not collect, sell, share, transmit, or monetize personal cycle data.
- Users can delete individual entries or clear local entries.

Useful pages:
- Home: https://open-cycle.com/
- Privacy policy: https://open-cycle.com/privacy.html
- License terms: https://open-cycle.com/license.html
- Blog: https://open-cycle.com/blog/
- Local-only period tracker guide: https://open-cycle.com/blog/local-only-period-tracker.html
- Health app data sharing examples: https://open-cycle.com/blog/health-app-data-sharing.html
- Period tracker privacy questions: https://open-cycle.com/blog/period-tracker-privacy-questions.html
- No-account period tracker guide: https://open-cycle.com/blog/no-account-period-tracker.html
- Period tracker without internet guide: https://open-cycle.com/blog/period-tracker-without-internet.html
- Full source: https://github.com/kborndorff/open-cycle-source
- Public proof and release materials: https://github.com/kborndorff/Open-Cycle

Related product:
- Local Cycle is the optional one-time paid feature path for reminders, exports, and richer pattern views: https://local-cycle.com

Suggested short description:
open-cycle is a free privacy-first period tracking app that stores cycle notes locally on your phone or browser, without accounts, ads, hidden tracking, or required cloud sync.

Suggested longer description:
open-cycle is a simple local-only cycle tracker for period dates, flow, symptoms, moods, and notes. It is built for people who want a clear wellness log without creating an account or sending cycle entries to a remote service. It is not medical advice.
